candidates.name: Daniel McKee
candidates.notes: 76th Governor of Rhode Island (2021-). Attended Assumption College (Catholic institution). Religion not publicly confirmed but likely Catholic. Signed sanctuary state executive order, drawing criticism from RI Catholic Conference. Progressive on social issues. Former Lt. Governor. Businessman from Cumberland.

profile.education: Assumption College (BA, Education & Political Science); Harvard Kennedy School (MPA)
profile.background: Small business owner and municipal leader in Cumberland; lieutenant governor before becoming 76th governor in 2021.

candidates.name: Jack Reed
candidates.notes: Senior Senator from Rhode Island (1997-). Roman Catholic. Heritage Action lifetime 3%. NRA F. West Point graduate, Army Ranger veteran. Ranking Member of Senate Armed Services Committee. Catholic but votes consistently progressive on social issues — pro-choice, supports gun control, backed Respect for Marriage Act.

profile.education: West Point (BS), Harvard Kennedy School (MPP), Harvard Law School (JD)
profile.background: Former Army Ranger and paratrooper. Former Rhode Island State Senator and U.S. House member. In Senate since 1997. Armed Services Committee chairman/ranking member.

district_offices.address: One Exchange Terrace, Suite 408, U.S. District Courthouse

aipac-track.title: TrackAIPAC — Rhode Island delegation
aipac-track.excerpt: Aggregates every AIPAC and pro-Israel-lobby contribution each member of Congress has received, sourced directly from FEC filings and OpenSecrets. Matches the RESOLUTE Citizen America-First question 3: foreign-backed lobby donations.

aipac-opensecrets.excerpt: Non-partisan campaign-finance database. Search the candidate's name to see every FEC-reported donor, including pro-Israel lobby contributions.

aipac-endorsements.excerpt: AIPAC's own public endorsement list. If a candidate appears here, they have accepted AIPAC backing; the RESOLUTE Citizen scoring criterion treats this as a failure on America-First question 3.

candidates.name: Sheldon Whitehouse
candidates.notes: Junior Senator from Rhode Island (2007-). Episcopalian. Heritage Action lifetime ~2%. NRA F. Former U.S. Attorney and Rhode Island Attorney General. Known for climate advocacy and 'dark money' in politics speeches. Consistently progressive voting record across all categories.

profile.education: Yale University (BA), University of Virginia School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Former Rhode Island Attorney General and U.S. Attorney. Son of a diplomat, grew up overseas. Leading climate change advocate in Senate. In Senate since 2007.
